Error codes that may be returned by CFNetServices functions or passed to CFNetServices callback functions.
A required argument was not provided.
The search or service was canceled.
An attempt was made to use a name that is already in use.
A search is already in progress.

A required configuration for local network access is missing.
Not used.
Resolution failed because the timeout was reached.

Access network services and handle changes in network configurations. Build on abstractions of network protocols to simplify tasks such as working with BSD sockets, administering HTTP and FTP servers, and managing Bonjour services.

Callback used to create a descriptive string representation of the data pointed to by `info`. In implementing this function, return a reference to a CFString object that describes your allocator and some characteristics of your user-defined data, which is used by `CFCopyDescription()`. You can set this field to `NULL`, in which case Core Foundation will provide a rudimentary description.

The callback used to create a descriptive string representation of the info pointer (or the data pointed to by the info pointer) for debugging purposes. This callback is called by the [CFCopyDescription(_:)](/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFCopyDescription(_:)) function.

Arbitrary pointer to user-allocated memory containing user-defined data that is associated with the service, browser, or monitor and is passed to their respective callback functions. The data must be valid for as long as the CFNetService, CFNetServiceBrowser, or CFNetServiceMonitor is valid. Set this field to `NULL` if your callback function doesn’t want to receive user-defined data.
An arbitrary pointer to allocated memory containing user-defined data that can be associated with the host and that is passed to the callbacks.

Initializes an object that contains user-defined data and callbacks for a network host.

Creates an object that contains user-defined data and callbacks for net service browsers using the specified values.
Initializes an object that contains user-defined data and callbacks for a network host using the specified values.
Specifies whether the resulting domain is the default registration or browse domain.
Specifies whether the result pertains to a search for domains or services.

Value is a `CFArray` of `CFString` objects indicating host name patterns that should bypass the proxy.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether simple host names are excluded. Simple host names are excluded if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether an FTP proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether an FTP proxy’s passive mode is enabled. The passive mode is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating the port number of an FTP proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object indicating the host name or IP number of an FTP proxy.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether a gopher proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` indicating the port number of a gopher proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object indicating the host name or IP number of a gopher proxy.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether an HTTP proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object containing the port number associated with the HTTP proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object containing the HTTP proxy host name or IP number.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether an HTTPS proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object containing the port number associated with the HTTPS proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object containing the HTTPS proxy host name or IP number.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether proxy autoconfiguration is enabled. Proxy autoconfiguration is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFString` object that contains the full JavaScript source of the ProxyAutoConfig (PAC) file.
Value is a `CFString` object that contains the URL of the proxy autoconfiguration (PAC) file.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether proxy autodiscovery is enabled. Proxy autodiscovery is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether an RTSP proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object containing the port number associated with the RTSP proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object containing the RTSP proxy host name or IP number.
Value is a `CFNumber` object indicating whether a SOCKS proxy is enabled. The proxy is enabled if the key is present and the associated value is nonzero.
Value is a `CFNumber` object containing the port number associated with the SOCKS proxy.
Value is a `CFString` object containing the SOCKS proxy host name or IP number.
Specifies that the proxy is determined by a provided autoconfiguration script.
Specifies that the proxy is determined by an autoconfiguration file at a given URL.
Specifies an FTP proxy.
Specifies an HTTP proxy.
Specifies an HTTPS proxy.
Specifies that no proxy should be used.
Specifies a SOCKS proxy.
The error code is an FTP error code.
The error code is an HTTP error code.
The error code is a Mach error code defined in `mach/error.h`.
The error code is an error code defined in `netdb.h`.
The error code is a `CFNetService` error code. For details, see the [CFNetServicesError](/documentation/cfnetwork/cfnetserviceserror) enumeration.
The error code is a system configuration error code as defined in `System/ConfigurationSystemConfiguration.h`.

When running CFNetwork code on Windows, this domain returns error codes associated with the underlying TCP/IP stack. You should also note that non-networking errors such as `ENOMEM` are delivered through the POSIX domain. See the header `winsock2.h` for relevant error codes.
Request rejected by the server because the client program and the `identd` daemon reported different user IDs.
Request rejected by the server because it couldn’t connect to the `identd` daemon on the client.
Request rejected by the server or request failed.
The SOCKS4 status code returned by the server.
The address returned is not of a known type. This error code is only valid for errors in the `kCFStreamErrorSOCKSSubDomainNone` subdomain.
The stream is not in a state that allows the requested operation. This error code is only valid for errors in the `kCFStreamErrorSOCKSSubDomainNone` subdomain..
The server’s desired negotiation method.
The response code that the server returned in reply to the connection request.
The status code that the server returned during authentication.
A general SOCKS error.
The version of SOCKS that the server wants to use.

The SOCKS server rejected access because it does not support connections with the requested SOCKS version. SOCKS client version. You can query the `kCFSOCKSVersionKey` key to find out what version the server requested. This error code is only valid for errors in the `kCFStreamErrorSOCKSSubDomainNone` subdomain.

The type of service for the stream. Providing the service type allows the system to properly handle certain attributes of the stream, including routing and suspension behavior. Most streams do not need to set this property. See [Stream Service Types](/documentation/CoreFoundation/stream-service-types) for a list of possible values.
A multimedia audio and video streaming service.
Specifies that the stream is a background download.
A call signaling service.
A responsive, time-sensitive, multimedia audio and video service.
A responsive, time-sensitive data service.
Specifies that the stream is providing interactive video data.
Specifies that the stream is providing VoIP service.
Specifies that the stream is providing interactive voice data.
A Boolean value that indicates whether connections may use the network when the user has specified Low Data Mode.
A Boolean value that indicates whether connections may use a network interface that the system considers expensive.
A boolean value indicating whether the stream is connected over a cellular (WWAN) interface. This is a read-only property, and is `false` until the connection has been established.
A Boolean value that indicates if the connection is using a network interface that the system considers expensive.

FTP File Transfer Offset stream property key for set and copy operations. The value of this property is a CFNumber of type `kCFNumberLongLongType` representing the file offset at which to start the transfer.
FTP Password stream property key for set and copy operations. A value of type CFString for storing the login password. Don’t set this property when anonymous FTP is desired.

FTP Proxy stream property key for set and copy operations. The property is a value of type CFDictionary that holds proxy dictionary key-value pairs. The dictionary returned by SystemConfiguration can also be set as the value of this property.
FTP Resource Size read stream property key copy operations. This property stores a CFNumber of type `kCFNumberLongLongType` representing the size of a resource in bytes.
FTP Passive Mode stream property key for set and copy operations. Set this property to `kCFBooleanTrue` to enable passive mode; set this property to `kCFBooleanFalse` to disable passive mode.
FTP User Name stream property key for set and copy operations. A value of type CFString for storing the login user name. Don’t set this property when anonymous FTP is desired.

HTTP Final Request property. A value of type CFHTTPMessage containing the final message transmitted by the stream after all modifications (including authentication, connection policy, redirects, and so on) have been made. This property cannot be set.
HTTP Final URL property. A value of type CFURL containing the final HTTP URL. This value differs from the URL in the original HTTP request if an autoredirection occurred. This property cannot be set.
HTTP Response Header property. When copied by [CFReadStreamCopyProperty(_:_:)](/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFReadStreamCopyProperty(_:_:)), the header of an HTTP response message is returned.
HTTP Should Auto Redirect property. Set this property to `kCFBooleanTrue` to enable autoredirection; set this property to `kCFBooleanFalse` to disable autoredirection.
A Boolean value indicating that the connection should not be established over a cellular (WWAN) connection. This value can only be set *before* you open the stream.
Proxy Local Bypass property key.
SSL Peer Certificates property key for copy operations, which return a `CFArray` object containing `SecCertificateRef` objects.
SSL Peer Trust property key for copy operations, which return a `SecTrustRef` object containing the result of the SSL handshake.
SSL Settings property key for set operations.
A Boolean value to request that the system keep a socket open and delays reclaiming it when the process moves to the background.
The key’s value is a `CFHostRef` for the remote host if it is known. If not, its value is `NULL`.
The key’s value is a `CFNetServiceRef` for the remote network service if it is known. If not, its value is `NULL`.
Security property key whose value indicates whether root certificates should be allowed.
Security property key whose value indicates whether expired certificates are allowed.
Security property whose value indicates whether expired root certificates are allowed.
Security property key whose value is a CFArray of SecCertificateRefs except for the first element in the array, which is a SecIdentityRef.
Security property key whose value indicates whether the connection is to act as a server in the SSL process.
Security property key whose value specifies the stream’s security level.
Security property key whose value overrides the name used for certificate verification.
Security property key whose value indicates whether the certificate chain should be validated.
The URL that caused the load to fail as a `CFURLRef` object.
The URL that caused the load to fail as a `CFStringRef` object.
The client and server couldn’t find a mutually agreeable authentication method.
A hint that the system will call the client’s callback function again soon.
Causes registrations to fail if a name conflict occurs.
Callback that removes a retain previously added for the service or browser on the `info` pointer. This field can be `NULL`, but setting this field to `NULL` may result in memory leaks.
The callback used to remove a retain previously added for the host on the info pointer.
Specifies whether the client should remove the result instead of adding it.

The callback used to add a retain for the service or browser using `info` for the life of the service or browser. This callback may be used for temporary references the service or browser needs to take. This callback returns the actual `info` pointer so it can be stored in the service or browser. This field can be `NULL`.

The callback used to add a retain for the host on the info pointer for the life of the host, and may be used for temporary references the host needs to take. This callback returns the actual info pointer to store in the host, almost always just the pointer passed as the parameter.
Version number for this structure. Currently the only valid value is zero.
The version number of the structure type passed as a parameter to the host client function. The only valid version number is `0`.
